entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 11 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 11 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-11 04:10AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Jason Roberts released from custody.
  • Australia news LIVE: Novak Djokovic defeats Nick Kyrgios to claim 21st grand slam title at Wimbledon; Aboriginal flag to fly atop of Harbour Bridge permanently
  • TikToker finds inheritance money and note in op shop handbag
  • Second Test, day four LIVE updates: Sri Lanka cop another COVID casualty, Aussies look for quick wickets
  • The world gets worse, but public libraries are forever
  • Judge commends Jason Roberts’ jury after ‘hardest’ case
  • Four people injured including three police officers in Gold Coast crash
  • Two dead after three ‘unrelated’ units catch fire across Sydney
  • First innings left Australia short: McDonald
  • NSW politics LIVE: Inquiry into John Barilaro’s US trade appointment hears from thwarted candidate
  • Anxiety grows for trapped harvest from Europe’s breadbasket
  • Why Leonard Cohen’s Hallelujah endures 56 years since it was written
  • ‘If we can move quickly, we will’: Jobs summit reforms could happen this year
  • Randwick Barracks accommodation proposal angers residents
  • Fittler to spring selection surprise for Origin decider amid prop shortage
  • Rachael Clancy's Hope In A Suitcase
  • COVID spreads through cruise ship docked in Brisbane
  • Prince George reacts while watching the Wimbledon men's final
  • ‘It was a bloodbath’: Police shoot Perth family’s dog, owners in hospital after attack
  • Public servant was told US trade job ‘a present for someone’, Barilaro inquiry hears
  • Best of cartoons, July 11, 2022
  • Global agenda to consider finance, compensation: climate envoy
  • Bowen urges Quad to collaborate, diversify and shore up energy security
  • Labor faces major climate test, under pressure from the Pacific
  • Word’s out: Labor’s brave new world comes with its own language
  • Building costs soar 10 per cent in a year with no slowdown in sight
  • Best of cartoons, July 10, 2022
  • National plan to end violence against women to top agenda
  • Boris Johnson is gone, but he leaves important lessons for Liberals
  • Another multibillion-dollar NSW train project at risk of delays, cost overruns
  • Unlocking Sydney’s history, one frame at a time
  • Trams to return to service within ‘a couple of months’ declares Spanish builder
  • NSW government cites national security to keep Barilaro documents secret
  • Church rejection of women misses chance to humanise
  • In the Herald: July 11, 1931
  • TV inspectors ruin a perfectly good Pilate
  • Why girls are shying away from HSC extension maths
  • Sydney’s flood risk equation must change, before it’s too late
  • Aboriginal flag to permanently replace NSW flag atop Sydney Harbour Bridge
  • ‘The worst is over’: Sydney set for rain reprieve but the damage is done
  • ‘Opening of a new world’: How international students are helping seniors to log on
  • America decided the pandemic was over. The coronavirus has other ideas
  • ‘Undeniable’ flaws in Abe’s security, Japan police admit as party secures election win
  • Steve Bannon, facing jail, agrees to testify to Jan 6 committee
  • Australian anti-communist activist Drew Pavlou evicted from Wimbledon final
  • From the Archives, 1972: The Match of the Century
  • China speaks on relations with Australia
  • Sri Lankan president and prime minister resign
  • ‘Go home Gota, thank you Cricket Australia’: Tourists bring some joy to a nation in crisis
  • Australia mourns Abe and should learn from his legacy
  • Storming the palace and then tidying up: Sri Lanka’s civil uprising
  • China blames Coalition government for ‘difficulties’ with Australia, proposes four-point plan
  • Ten years on from the God particle, we may have found a fifth force of nature
  • Dutch farmers bring towns to a standstill in backlash against emissions reductions
  • Suspect blamed Abe for mother’s bankruptcy, used ‘simple to make’ DIY gun
  • Britain is falling apart, and it could finish the Tories
  • Body found at Brisbane train station
  • Man shot dead in Melbourne's south-east
  • Jason Roberts found not guilty
  • Silk, Miller families ‘devastated’ after Jason Roberts found not guilty of murdering police officers
  • WA’s quarantine facility still has no use one month before completion
  • Monday madness at airports
  • Victoria to restrict deals silencing sexual harassment victims
  • The conflicting accounts Roberts, Debs and prosecutors gave of Silk-Miller shootings
  • A mass migration: record numbers arrive in WA during pandemic
  • Man’s body found at Fortitude Valley station
  • Perth, why can’t you provide apartments families want to live in?
  • The problem with Elon Musk’s high stakes plan to pull out of $US44bn Twitter deal
  • Property services group Link rejects $2.3b Dye & Durham takeover offer
  • ASX dips despite following Wall Street’s winning week
  • ‘Kill switch’: Uber used stealth tech, lobbied world leaders to avoid regulation: report
  • How social investing is changing the way Australians trade
  • Politico mulls possible foray into Australian media landscape
  • More exits at embattled journalism institute as it pauses international programs
  • Local media companies inject cash into Catalano’s new real-estate play
  • Livista Energy eyes Australian lithium producers to secure Europe’s EV battery supplies
  • ‘We cannot retreat’: Businesses warn a return to work from home won’t work for them
  • Lettuce seeds, refurbished tech boom at eBay as shoppers bargain hunt
  • Reserve Bank charts path to higher interest rates, but there may be potholes ahead
  • The little Shanghai girl who dared to dream: Meet the Australian $1b fund manager
  • Clean energy demand to drive copper price higher in future, despite recent falls
  • Quick Q: Who and what Foxtel’s content boss most admires
  • Want to learn more about Roe v Wade? Here’s the podcast for you
  • Australian podcasters sign exclusive, multimillion-dollar deal with Spotify
  • Trailer for new Aussie Netflix series 'Surviving Summer'
  • ‘This is my story’: Sydney’s controversial rappers defend their music
  • Off-screen and on, Jeff Bridges faces his own mortality
  • Don't Worry Darling (2022) | Official Trailer
  • What makes Dorian Gray such a hit? The music is part of the secret
  • We need to talk about Russell Crowe’s accent in Thor (and everything else)
  • Magic spells, goats and fire: A most confusing night at the theatre
  • ‘Young, funny, irritatingly handsome’: Euan’s challenge in taking on Joseph
  • National weather forecast for Monday July 11
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 11 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 11 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-11 03:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • As fuel prices shoot up, Hong Kong taxi drivers work smarter, other motorists choose to leave the car at home
  • Hong Kong housing chief vows to locate more land for transitional homes after visit to subdivided flats and ‘cage homes’

  • In Taiwan, Shinzo Abe’s death means loss of steadfast supporter

  • How Indian royals from Rajasthan to Orissa are helping revive traditional arts and crafts
  • Asia faces a ‘rice crisis’ but China and India may escape the worst price rises, analysts say
  • Secretary of State Blinken vows pressure on Myanmar junta, urges China to do the same, during trip to Thailand
  • Uber lobbied politicians, broke laws in global push: investigation
  • French opposition denounces reports of ‘secret deal’ between Uber and Emmanuel Macron
  • UK foreign minister Liz Truss joins 11-strong race to replace Boris Johnson
  • Ghana fears Nigeria jail break jihadists may try to enter country
  • Ukraine ‘deeply disappointed’ after Canada exempts Russian gas turbine from sanctions
  • Israeli ministers Gideon Saar and Benny Gantz to run as partners in upcoming elections
  • Novak Djokovic sees off Nick Kyrgios to win seventh Wimbledon title and 21st grand slam
  • Faroe Islands limits killing of dolphins to 500 a year after anger over annual hunt
  • South Africa gunmen kill 19 people ‘randomly’ in two bar shootings, say police
  • Russian rockets kill 15 in block of flats in Ukraine’s Donbas, say officials
  • Hong Kong stocks see sharpest drop in two weeks amid Shanghai, Macau Covid-19 flare-ups, fines on Alibaba, Tencent, Bilibili
  • Swap Connect with Hong Kong will help China attract more international investors to its bond markets, Standard Chartered banker says
  • Green stocks such as BYD and CATL are red hot again, as Chinese stocks flirt with bull market
  • As nuclear power makes a comeback, China is poised to be the net-zero hero
  • Macau to shut casinos for first time in two years as China’s gambling hub moves to curb rising number of new Covid-19 cases
  • Smallest flats see brisk demand at first Hong Kong home sale after Xi speech underlining desire for larger homes
  • To bridge divide at heart of US-China tensions, start with global rethink of public sector’s role
  • Carbon capture and storage tech too risky an investment in absence of quality data and low commercial readiness, report finds
  • Law governing cryptocurrency will boost Hong Kong’s leap into virtual asset market
  • Why school leadership is a driving force for innovations in education
  • What is ‘face blindness’ and why does Brad Pitt say people hate him because of it?
  • Abortion in ancient China with TCM and acupuncture was unreliable – most women resorted to physically injuring themselves, as in one harrowing story
  • Why most overseas visitors to Japan are on business trips, with the country opening up cautiously
  • 5 Bali wellness experiences, from light therapy to breathwork, put to the test
  • Shenzhen in China becomes first mainland city to make ‘living wills’ into law covering end-of-life care
  • Alopecia areata, Jada Pinkett Smith’s hair-loss condition, now treatable by arthritis and Covid-19 drug, heralding ‘a new era’ for sufferers
  • Are Scientology founder L. Ron Hubbard’s far-fetched tales in China and Asia diaries actually true - at least partly?
  • Why ex-Lonely Planet publisher switched to counselling: Simon Westcott on death, and being abused by a teacher
  • Meet Sonoya Mizuno, star of the Game of Thrones prequel: she danced alongside Emma Stone in La La Land and modelled for an Adidas campaign, but most will know her from Crazy Rich Asians
  • Meet Alastair Wallace Stewart, Rod Stewart’s dashing son: he’s following in mum Penny Lancaster’s footsteps, modelling for Italian fashion brand Palm Angels – but he also loves swimming and football
  • Saint Seb! 5 times Sebastian Vettel proved he’s the nicest guy in Formula One: from campaigning for LGBT rights and supporting Ukraine, to reforming motorsports to combat climate change
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 11 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • British tourists survive avalanche in Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan
  • Russia-Ukraine war update: what we know on day 138 of the invasion

  • Shinzo Abe: Japanese voters back party of former PM amid shooting fallout

  • Have a biscuit and shut up ... now psychologists have found ‘hanger’ is a real thing, here’s how to deal with it
  • Ithaka review – intriguing portrait of the campaign to free Julian Assange
  • Kiribati withdraws from Pacific Islands Forum in blow to regional body
